definition.en: Identities may be termed action which is conscious of itself.
definition.zh: 身份可被称为对自身有意识的行动。

definition.en: Identity is also a dimension of existence, action within action, an unfolding of action upon itself; and through this interweaving of action with itself, through this reaction, an identity is formed.
definition.zh: 身份也是存在的一个维度，行动中的行动，行动对自身的展开；通过行动与自身的这种交织，通过这种反作用，一个身份得以形成。

definition.en: Though you are a portion of your psyche, then, your identity is still inviolate. It will not be submerged or annihilated in a greater self. It carries a stamp -- a divine mark -- of its own integrity.
definition.zh: 尽管你是你心灵的一部分，你的身份仍然是不可侵犯的。它不会被淹没或消灭在一个更大的自我中。它携带着自身完整性的印记——一个神圣的标记。

definition.en: The energy of the action, the workings of action within and upon itself, forms identity.
definition.zh: 行动的能量，行动在自身内部及对自身的运作，形成了身份。

definition.en: All of an individual's experiences, even those of which he is not aware on a conscious basis, therefore are part of the electromagnetic reality that forms this particular individual's electromagnetic identity.
definition.zh: 因此，一个个体的所有经验，甚至那些他在意识层面上没有察觉到的，都是形成这个特定个体电磁身份的那个电磁实相的一部分。

explanation: 身份（Identity）是意识到自身存在的行动。它不是由身体、性别角色或社会框架定义的固定边界，而是一个动态的、不断自我更新的过程。身份不需要任何结构来支撑，它本质上是一种心理环境，而非物理实体。你的身份具有不可侵犯的完整性，即使它只是更大意识的一部分，也绝不会被淹没或消灭。身份通过行动与自身的交织、反应而形成，并且每个身份都保留着其独特的核心印记。身份可以与其他身份融合，但永远不会变得比原来更少。真正的身份不依赖于物质生存，也不依赖于肉体导向的自我（ego）的延续。它存在于所有部分的自我之中，是永恒且不可分解的。

quotes.text: Since you value sexual performance in the most limited of terms, and use that largely as a focus of identity, then both your old and young suffer consequences that are not so much the result of age as of sexual prejudice. It is interesting to note that both the old and the young also find themselves outside of your organizational frameworks. The young are more freewheeling in their thoughts before they accept sexual roles, and the old are more freewheeling in theirs because they have discarded their sexual roles. I did not say that old or young had no sexual expression -- but that both groups did not identify their identities with their sexual roles. There are of course exceptions. If the man or the woman is taught that identity is a matter of sexual performance, however, and that that performance must cease at a certain age, then the sense of identity can also begin to disintegrate. If children feel that identity is dependent upon such performance, then they will begin to perform as quickly as possible. They will squeeze their identity into sexual clothes, and the society will suffer because the great creative thrusts of growing intellect and intuitions will be divided at puberty, precisely when they are needed.
quotes.translation: 由于你们以最狭隘的标准来评价性表现，并将其作为身份认同的主要焦点，那么无论老人还是年轻人都将承受后果——这些后果与其说是年龄所致，不如说是性偏见所致。有趣的是，老人和年轻人同样发现自己被排除在你们的社会组织框架之外。年轻人在接受性别角色之前思想更为自由奔放，而老年人则因摒弃了性别角色而思想更为无拘无束。我并非说老人或年轻人没有性表达——而是说这两个群体都不以性别角色来定义自身身份。当然也有例外。如果男人或女人被教导身份认同取决于性表现，且这种表现必须在某个年龄终止，那么身份感也会开始瓦解。如果孩子们认为身份认同依赖于这种表现，他们就会尽快开始表现。他们会将身份挤入性别的外衣，而社会将因此受害——因为正在成长的智识与直觉的伟大创造力，恰在青春期被分裂，而此刻正是最需要它们的时候。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You think that the self must begin or end someplace. There must be a fence around it, a yard of identity in which you can feel safe. I have said many times that there are no limitations to the self. You seem to be afraid that the self will bleed out and lose "itself" in a maze in which all identity is lost. Yet you recognize that your self is a far greater dimension than you usually suppose, so you speak in terms of reincarnation. This allows you to imagine greater realms of identity while still holding your concepts of selfhood intact. You think of being one self after another, each identity being neatly separated from the others by a passage of years, an obvious death and an obvious birth.
quotes.translation: 你们认为自我必须有起点或终点。它周围必须有道篱笆，有个能让你们感到安全的身份庭院。我多次说过，自我没有局限。你们似乎害怕自我会渗漏出去，在一切身份都迷失的迷宫中失去"自身"。然而你们又承认自己的自我远比通常想象的维度更广阔，所以你们用转世轮回的说法来谈论。这使你们在保持自我概念完整的同时，仍能想象更宏大的身份领域。你们认为自己是接续不断的各个自我，每个身份都被岁月的流逝、明确的死亡与明确的出生彼此整齐分隔。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 729
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Identities may be termed action which is conscious of itself. For the purposes of our discussion, the terms action and identity must be separated. However basically no such separation exists, for an identity is also a dimension of existence, action within action, an unfolding of action upon itself; and through this interweaving of action with itself, through this reaction, an identity is formed.
quotes.translation: 身份可被定义为对自身具有觉知的行动。为便于讨论，我们必须将"行动"与"身份"这两个术语分开。然而根本上并不存在这种分离，因为身份也是存在的一个维度——行动中的行动，行动对自身的展开；正是通过行动与自身的交织，通过这种反作用，身份得以形成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In metaphysical terms, you have your being in your psyche or soul in somewhat the same manner. Identities are obviously psychic environments, primarily, rather than physical ones. Physical objects cannot move through each other, as a table cannot move through a chair. Mental events behave differently. They can mix and merge, move through each other while still maintaining their own focus. They can interact on psychic levels in the way that events do on physical levels, but without physical restrictions. Though you are a portion of your psyche, then, your identity is still inviolate. It will not be submerged or annihilated in a greater self. It carries a stamp -- a divine mark -- of its own integrity. It follows its own focus, and knows itself as itself, even while its own existence as itself may be but a portion of another "identity."
quotes.translation: 在形而上层面，你们以类似的方式存在于自己的心灵或灵魂中。身份显然是心灵环境，而非物理环境。物理对象无法彼此穿透，就像桌子不能穿过椅子。心灵事件的行为则不同。它们可以混合交融，彼此穿透而仍保持自身焦点。它们能在心灵层面相互作用，如同事件在物理层面那样，却没有物理限制。因此，虽然你们是心灵的一部分，你们的身份依然神圣不可侵犯。它不会被更大的自我淹没或消灭。它带着自身完整性的印记——神圣的烙印。它遵循自身焦点，知晓自身即为自身，即便其作为自身的存有可能只是另一个"身份"的一部分。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The reality of such an identity then exists within the action. The energy of the action, the workings of action within and upon itself, forms identity. There are other causes here that we will consider later. Yet although identity is formed from action, action and identity cannot be separated. You will remember our previous definition of action, for this will make this evening's discussion easier to understand.
quotes.translation: 这种身份的现实性存在于行动之内。行动的能量、行动在自身内部及对自身的作用，构成了身份。这里还有其他原因，我们稍后会探讨。然而尽管身份由行动形成，行动与身份却不可分离。你们会记得我们之前对行动的定义，这将使今晚的讨论更易理解。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: But identity is much more than this, and basic identity, while using the perceptive abilities, is not that dependent upon them. It is true that the personality is a gestalt, and that every identity has any number of potential egos. It is also true that on occasion one potential ego will take over from another. But this is all highly simplified, for the ego structure is not one thing, but a changing, never constant, actually quite informal grouping of psychological patterns. Each ego uses and interprets the organisms perceiving apparatus in a way that in the overall is characteristic and distinctive.
quotes.translation: 但身份远不止于此，基本身份虽然运用感知能力，却并不那么依赖它们。确实，人格是完形结构，每个身份都拥有无数潜在自我。确实，有时一个潜在自我会接替另一个。但这都是高度简化的说法，因为自我结构并非单一实体，而是不断变化、永无常形、实际上相当非正式的心理模式集合。每个自我都以整体上独特而鲜明的方式运用和诠释有机体的感知机制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: A psychic gestalt is dependent upon matter, not for its identity but merely for its survival in the physical plane. Psychic gestalts or identities or individualities are for all practical purposes immortal. They may join other gestalts but they will never be less than they once were. Identity then is never broken down. Any apparent breaking down is never an actual fact, as the personality could be thought of as a breaking down of the entity; but this is not so. The personality did not exist as such before its creation by the entity, and once it becomes an identity, it retains that individuality.
quotes.translation: 心灵完形依赖物质，并非为了其身份，而仅仅是为了在物质层面的存续。心灵完形或身份或个体性，实际上是不朽的。它们可能与其他完形结合，但绝不会比它们曾经的样子更少。因此，身份永远不会被分解。任何看似分解的情况都不是事实，正如人格可以被视为存有的分解，但事实并非如此。人格在被存有创造之前并不存在，而一旦它成为一个身份，它就保留了那种个体性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: Again, it may not involve motion in terms with which you usually refer. There are many kinds of motion, for example, that you do not perceive as motion within the physical field. Action tampers with identity, yet were it not for action identity would be impossible. It may, here, sound like a contradiction; but to remain an identity, an identity must completely renew itself, and each renewal is indeed a termination. Yet without the termination no new action on the part of the identity would be possible. And without action no identity can be aware of its own existence.
quotes.translation: 同样，它可能不涉及你通常所指的运动。有许多种运动，例如，你在物质场中并不感知为运动。行动会篡改身份，然而，若非行动，身份就不可能存在。在这里，这听起来可能像是一个矛盾；但要保持一个身份，身份必须完全更新自己，而每一次更新确实是一次终结。然而，没有终结，身份方面的新行动就不可能发生。而没有行动，身份就无法意识到自己的存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: All of an individual's experiences, even those of which he is not aware on a conscious basis, therefore are part of the electromagnetic reality that forms this particular individual's electromagnetic identity. It exists within the physical matter of the organism during existence within the physical system. While the experiences which form this framework and compose this individual's identity are obtained through his interaction with the physical system, his electromagnetic identity is not dependent upon the physical field.
quotes.translation: 因此，一个个体的所有经历，甚至那些他意识层面上不知道的经历，都是形成这个特定个体电磁身份的电性实相的一部分。在物质系统内的存在期间，它存在于有机体的物质中。虽然构成这个框架并组成这个个体身份的经历是通过他与物质系统的互动获得的，但他的电磁身份并不依赖于物质场。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: The energy that composes personality therefore consists of an inconceivable number of separate identities. These separate identities form what we call the inner self, which retains its individuality even while the energy that composes it constantly changes. There are continual groupings and regroupings, but basic identities are always retained. The potential egos within any given identity therefore retain their own individuality and self-knowledge, regardless of their relative importance in the order of command.
quotes.translation: 因此，构成人格的能量包含了数量难以想象的独立身份。这些独立身份形成了我们所说的内在自己，即使构成它的能量不断变化，它也保留其个体性。存在着持续的分组和重组，但基本身份始终被保留。因此，任何给定身份内的潜在自我都保留着自己的个体性和自我认知，无论它们在指挥顺序中的相对重要性如何。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The inner senses collect information of which the conscious self may not be aware. The astral identity of course is aware of communications from both the inner and outer environment. The astral identity is therefore actually a more complete representation of the whole personality, and its abilities are far-reaching. The inner ego of which we have spoken is the director of this astral identity. You should see now how this fits in with some of our older material.
quotes.translation: 内在感官收集意识自我可能不知道的信息。星光身份当然知道来自内在和外在环境的沟通。因此，星光身份实际上是整个人格更完整的代表，其能力深远。我们所说的内在自我是这星光身份的指导者。你现在应该看到这如何与我们的一些旧资料相吻合。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Feelings and sensations and memories that it knows nothing of have built up your psychological identity, and given you a sense of continuity. Of these this ego knows but little. Intimacy with your self and with your own identity, that is intimate knowledge of your own identity, resides in all portions of the self, and is not dependent upon a survival based upon the survival of the physically-oriented ego. This would represent a sham of a survival indeed.
quotes.translation: 它不知道的感觉、感受和记忆已经构建了你的心理身份，并给了你一种连续感。这个自我对此知之甚少。与你自己和你自己的身份的亲密，即对你自己的身份的亲密知识，存在于自我的所有部分中，并不依赖于基于物质导向自我的生存。那确实会是一种虚假的生存。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Since it is itself action, such an attempt is basically doomed to failure. Yet the very attempt causes the formation of the ego. Once this apparent separate ego is formed, and once a fair amount of stability is maintained, and a new identity arrived at, the initial desire and energy will maintain the ego in its position during its existence in any field. Since this existence of separate identity is assured, attempts should then be made so that the ego can better participate in its realization of action, and the emotional life is very important in this respect.
quotes.translation: 由于它本身就是行动，这种尝试基本上注定失败。然而正是这种尝试导致了自我的形成。一旦这个看似独立的自我形成，并维持了相当程度的稳定性，获得了一个新的身份，最初的欲望和能量就会在任何领域中维持自我的位置。既然这种独立身份的存在得到了保证，就应该尝试让自我更好地参与其对行动的认知，而情感生活在这方面非常重要。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Self structures and identities are not the same. Identity requires no structure. Identity knows, and knows that it knows. self structures know and do not know that they know. self structures are a part of identity. Identities can contain pure knowledge without translation, and use it to seed various existences and to form realities. They do this consciously in your terms. They are far more conscious than you can presently imagine.
quotes.translation: 自我结构与身份并不相同。身份不需要结构。身份知道，并且知道它知道。自我结构知道，却不知道它们知道。自我结构是身份的一部分。身份可以包含未经翻译的纯粹知识，并用它来播种各种存在并形成实相。以你们的说法，它们是有意识地这样做的。它们比你们目前所能想象的要有意识得多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... (Then slowly:) It is difficult to explain on spiritual and psychic levels without speaking in terms of gradations of identity, for example, but in your terms even the smallest "particle" of identity is inviolate. It may grow, develop or expand, change alliances or organizations, and it does combine with others even as cells do. (Long pause.) Your body does not feel as if you invade it. Your consciousness and its consciousness are merged; yet it is composed of the multitudinous individual consciousnesses that form the tiniest physical particles within it. Those particles come and go, yet your body remains itself. What was physically a part of you last year is not today. Physically, you are a different person. Put simply, the stuff of the body is constantly returned to the earth,* where it forms again into physical actualization -- but always differently.
quotes.translation: 请给我们一点时间……（然后缓慢地：）在灵性与心灵层面，如果不以身份等级来表述，很难解释清楚。但以你们的说法，即使是最微小的身份"粒子"也是不可侵犯的。它可以成长、发展或扩展，改变联盟或组织，并且确实像细胞一样与其他结合。（长停顿。）你的身体不会让你感觉仿佛入侵了它。你的意识与它的意识是融合的；然而它是由无数个体意识组成的，这些意识形成了其中最小的物理粒子。那些粒子来来去去，但你的身体仍然保持自身。去年你身体的一部分，今天已不复存在。从物理上讲，你是一个不同的人。简单来说，身体的物质不断回归大地，在那里再次形成物理显化——但总是以不同的方式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 725
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: And yet a portion of you knows the answer, and a portion of you knows who you are, and the memories of your previous lives are not in your genes or in your chromosomes, but in the psychic reality that forms the genes and the chromosomes. For the identity is a different identity than the name that you bear. The physical mechanism has chromosomes indeed, but the physical chromosomes have a psychic counterpart, and the psychic counterpart is the original; and within you is the codified information containing all your past lives and all your knowledge, and it is hidden so deep within you that the subconscious as you know it does not realize the truth, for the subconscious as you know it is, indeed, a very shallow affair containing only those hidden memories from this life.
quotes.translation: 然而你的一部分知道答案，你的一部分知道你是谁，你前世的记忆不在你的基因或染色体中，而是在形成基因和染色体的心灵实相中。因为身份与你所承载的名字是不同的身份。物理机制确实有染色体，但物理染色体有一个心灵对应物，而心灵对应物才是原版；在你之内包含着编码信息，包含你所有前世和所有知识，它隐藏得如此之深，以至于你所知的潜意识并不了解真相，因为你所知的潜意识实际上是一件非常浅薄的东西，只包含这一生隐藏的记忆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: (From the 775th session for May 10, 1976:) Bits of your consciousnesses,34 Joseph and Ruburt, go out through these books. I am not speaking symbolically. Those portions will mix with the consciousness of others. Portions of your intent and purpose become theirs. My own psychological reality is not particleized. My identity includes the identities of many others, and they each operate in their own fashion. In those terms I am a wave formation. More specifically, however, and to a lesser degree, each physical person operates partially as a particleized being, and partially in terms of a wave.35 But identity, being itself inviolate, is on the other hand everchanging -- and there is, in the larger framework of reality, no contradiction.
quotes.translation: （摘自1976年5月10日第775节：）你们意识的一部分，约瑟夫和鲁柏，通过这些书散发出去。我不是在象征性地说话。那些部分会与别人的意识混合。你们意图和目的的一部分成为他们的。我自己的心理实相并非粒子化。我的身份包含许多其他人的身份，他们各自以各自的方式运作。用那些术语来说，我是一个波结构。但更具体地说，在较小程度上，每个物理人部分地作为粒子化存在运作，部分地以波的方式运作。但身份本身是不可侵犯的，另一方面又是不断变化的——在实相的更大框架中，这并不矛盾。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Now I'll refer the reader to Note 6 for Session 711, which is the session for which I'm assembling this chronology to begin with. In that note I presented some material on ancient connections with the name, Seth, then quoted Seth on the subject of names from the ESP class session for April 17, 1973. For instance:)
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Any section of the land has an identity, so to speak, and I am not talking symbolically. Such identities represent the combined organizations of consciousness of land, man, and animal, within any given realm. Simply enough put, there are as many kinds of consciousnesses as there are particles, and these are combined in infinite fashions. (Long pause.) In the dream state some of that experience, otherwise closed to you, forms the background of the dream drama (A one-minute pause.) Your consciousness is not one thing like a flashlight, that you possess. It is instead a literally endless conglomeration of points of consciousness, swarming together to form your validity -- stamped, as it were, with your identity.
quotes.translation: 可以说，任何一片土地都有一个身份，我不是在象征性地说话。这些身份代表了任何特定领域中土地、人类和动物的意识组合组织。简单来说，有多少粒子就有多少种意识，它们以无限的方式组合。（长停顿。）在梦境状态中，一些否则对你封闭的经验形成了梦境戏剧的背景（一分钟停顿。）你的意识不是像手电筒那样你拥有的单一东西。相反，它是一个真正无尽的意识点集合体，蜂拥在一起形成你的有效性——可以说，被打上了你身份的印记。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: We will continue our discussion concerning action and identities. I have said that identity is a part of action, and basically inseparable from action. Identity attempts to form meaningful patterns and relationships from action. Consciousness is action that perceives itself. The ego is action's attempt to stand off from itself.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续讨论关于行动与身份认同的话题。我曾说过，身份认同是行动的一部分，且本质上与行动不可分割。身份认同试图从行动中构建出有意义的模式与关系。意识是能够感知自身的行动。自我则是行动试图与自身保持距离的产物。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You apply this belief to physical systems and psychological ones. In terms of sex, you insist upon a picture that shows you a growth into a sexual identity, a clear focus, and then in old age a falling away of clear sexual identification into "sexual disorder." It does not occur to you that the original premise or focus, the identification of identity with sexual nature, is "unnatural." It is you, then, who form the entire framework from which your judgment is made. In many cases the person is truer to his or her own identity in childhood or old age, when greater individual freedom is allowed, and sexual roles are more flexible.
quotes.translation: 你将这种信念应用于物理系统与心理系统。在性别方面，你坚持一种图景：成长过程中形成清晰的性别认同，到老年时这种清晰的性别认同又瓦解为"性别混乱"。你从未想过，将身份认同与性别本质等同起来的原始前提或焦点，本身就是"不自然的"。正是你构建了做出判断的整个框架。在许多情况下，人在童年或老年时期反而更接近其真实身份，因为那时允许更大的个人自由，性别角色也更为灵活。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What I am saying here applies to the greater identity of each reader. Give us a moment ... Because you are usually so worried about preserving what you think of as your identity, we use terms like reincarnational selves or counterparts. If you truly understood the nature of your individuality, however, you would clearly see that there is no contradiction if I say that you are uniquely yourself, that your individuality has an indestructible validity that is never assailed, and when I also say that you are at the same time connected with other identities, each as sacredly inviolate as your own.
quotes.translation: 我在此所言适用于每位读者的更大身份。请稍等片刻……由于你们通常过于担忧保护自认为的身份，我们使用了诸如转世自我或对应体之类的术语。然而，若你真正理解个体性的本质，就会清楚看到，当我说你是独一无二的自己、你的个体性具有永不受损的不可摧毁有效性，同时又说你与其他身份相连（每个身份都如你自身般神圣不可侵犯）时，这并不矛盾。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 740
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Now you are merged with a concept, now, of what you are. So merged that you cannot see your way clearly out of the concept, nor easily imagine reality from any other viewpoint but the self structure that you presently imagine yourself to be. (Pause.) Another part of your whole identity is quite aware that you are delving into one concept of yourself. To delve into it, you gladly and willingly momentarily forget the greater portions of yourself. You experience the concept fully. Your primary identity is quite aware of other self concepts that are also being experienced. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 此刻你已与关于自身的一个概念融为一体。如此融合以至于你无法清晰看到脱离此概念的道路，也难以从你当前所想象的自我结构之外的任何视角轻易想象实相。（停顿）你整体身份的另一部分完全清楚你正在深入探索关于自身的一个概念。为了深入探索，你欣然且自愿地暂时遗忘自身更大部分。你充分体验这个概念。你的首要身份完全清楚还有其他自我概念正在被体验。（停顿）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: The complicated organism which is the human personality with its physical structure, has evolved, along with many other structures, a highly differentiated "I" consciousness, whose very nature is such that it attempts to preserve the apparent boundaries of identity. To do so it chooses between actions, for the very choice, or act of choosing, and ability to do so, represents the nature of identity. But beneath this sophisticated gestalt are the simpler foundations of its being, and indeed the very acceptance of all stimuli without which identity would be impossible.
quotes.translation: 人类人格这一复杂有机体及其生理结构，与众多其他结构共同进化出高度分化的"我"意识，其本质特性在于试图维护身份认同的明显边界。为此，它在行动之间做出选择，因为选择本身（或选择的行为）及选择能力正代表了身份认同的本质。但在这精妙完形之下，是其存在的更简单基础——实际上正是对所有刺激的接纳，若无此接纳，身份认同便不可能存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Now. At some point you, Joseph, and Ruburt and myself, are part of the same entity. This entity is that synthesization that Dunne did not foresee, but it in no way implies a loss of individual identities. This is extremely difficult to explain, since when I use the word individual identity, I am not referring primarily to egotistical identity alone. As a matter of fact, I am in one way, and in one way only, a future self--this is extremely simplified--of Ruburts; that could be compared I suppose to a theoretical number twelve self, according to Dunne.
quotes.translation: 现在。在某个层面上，你约瑟夫、鲁柏和我本人，都是同一实体的组成部分。这个实体是邓恩未能预见的那种综合，但它绝不意味着个体身份的丧失。这极难解释，因为当我使用"个体身份"一词时，我主要指的并非仅自我身份。事实上，从某种角度且仅从这一角度而言，我是鲁柏的一个未来自我——这已极度简化——若按邓恩的理论，大概可类比为第十二号自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: But beyond this life, and before this life, and forming this life, there are identities and there are realities and these are not dead. Identity as experience is intimate. It is a psychic reality but it exists electromagnetically. Identity, the self that you are, is made up of the selves that you were and, my dear friends, the selves that you shall be. The selves that you were still exist. The selves that you shall be already exist. The lives that you have lived, in your terms, are still being lived. There is no past, present or future.
quotes.translation: 但超越此生、在此生之前、并形成此生的，存在着身份与实相，而这些并非死寂。作为经验的身份是亲密的。它是一种心灵实相，却以电磁方式存在。身份——你所是的自己——由你曾是的自己组成，而亲爱的朋友们，也由你将是 的自己组成。你曾是的自己依然存在。你将是 的自己早已存在。按你们的说法，你们活过的生命仍在继续。没有过去、现在或未来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Action may show itself as motion, but it is much more than motion in the terms which you usually use, and motion is but one small dimension within actions realm. All types of consciousness represent a different focus of energy's perception within itself. There is no past or future to action. All action is simultaneous. Identities, some identities and some forms of consciousness, particularly the ego, perceive a past or a present, but this is merely the result of the manner in which such identities and consciousness view available data.
quotes.translation: 行动可能表现为运动，但它远不止于你们通常所说的运动，而运动只是行动领域内一个微小的维度。所有类型的意识都代表能量在其自身内部感知的不同焦点。行动没有过去或未来。所有行动都是同时发生的。身份——某些身份和某些形式的意识，尤其是自我——会感知到过去或现在，但这仅仅是这类身份和意识看待可用数据的方式所导致的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Any deep exploration of the self will lead you into areas that will confound conventional beliefs about sexuality. You will discover an identity, a psychological and psychic identity, that is in your terms male and female, one in which those abilities of each sex are magnified, released, and expressed. They may not be so released in normal life, but you will meet the greater dimensions of your own reality, and at least in the dream state catch a glimpse of the self that transcends a one-sex orientation.
quotes.translation: 任何对自我的深入探索都会将你带入那些会颠覆关于性别的传统信念的领域。你会发现一个身份——一个心理与心灵的身份——按你们的说法是既男又女的，其中两性的能力都被放大、释放和表达。这些能力在正常生活中可能不会被释放，但你会遇见自己实相的更大维度，至少在梦境状态中会瞥见那超越单一性别取向的自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The personality structure does not make sense unless such past experience is taken into consideration. Potentials do not simply appear, they evolve. I have told you that the most minute portion of energy possesses consciousness and has its own identity. This identity of itself is never annihilated. It may form into new gestalts but the identity is retained.
quotes.translation: 除非将这种过去的经验纳入考量，否则人格结构便毫无意义。潜能并非凭空出现，它们会演化。我曾告诉你们，最微小的能量单位也拥有意识并保有其自身身份。这身份本身永远不会被消灭。它可能形成新的完形，但身份会被保留。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Identity then, is action's effect upon itself. Without identities action would be meaningless, for there would be nothing upon which action could act. Action must, therefore, of its very nature, of itself and from its own workings, create identities. Again, action and identity cannot be separated. This applies from the most simple to the most complex.
quotes.translation: 因此，身份是行动对其自身的作用。没有身份，行动将毫无意义，因为将没有任何东西可供行动作用其上。因此，行动按其本性，必然从自身、从其自身的运作中创造出身份。再次强调，行动与身份不可分离。这适用于从最简单到最复杂的一切。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: In those terms each living person has other counterparts of himself or herself alive, generally speaking, at the same time, sharing the physical face of the earth. There are psychic pools of identity, therefore; and generally speaking those alive in any given century are as much a part of that inner pool as they are a part of the particular race to which they may belong. Each member of the species is an individual, and each member of a psychic pool of identity is an individual.
quotes.translation: 用那些话来说，每个活着的人通常都有其自身的其他对应体同时活着，共享地球的物理面貌。因此存在着身份的心灵池；而一般来说，任何特定世纪中活着的人，既是该内在池的一部分，也是他们可能所属的特定种族的一部分。物种的每个成员都是个体，而身份心灵池的每个成员也都是个体。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 722
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The limits of identity are arbitrary on your part, developed throughout the stages of your evolutionary process, not for any reason inherent in identity itself, but merely for purely practical reasons on your physical field, having to do with the amount of matter that various kinds of identities could effectively manipulate and control.
quotes.translation: 身份的限制是你们自行设定的，在你们进化过程的各个阶段中发展出来，并非出于身份本身的任何内在原因，而纯粹是基于你们物质场域的实际考量，与各类身份能有效操控和掌控的物质数量有关。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: These potential egos at one time or another will have their chance, as dominant egos, in this existence or in another reincarnation. They represent the overall potentials of the whole identity in respect to physical existence. The identity has in other words latent abilities which it will not use within the physical system, but all of the latent ability ever available lies within the original identity.
quotes.translation: 这些潜在的自我，在此时或彼时，将有机会作为主导自我，在这一世或另一世转世中显现。它们代表了整个身份在物质存在方面的整体潜能。换言之，身份拥有它不会在物质系统内使用的潜在能力，但所有曾经可用的潜在能力都存在于原始身份之中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: The identity, the basic identity, of these portions of the self, are carried by what you could compare to the subconscious that you know. This is difficult, but listen: in these portions of the self it is the subconscious that carries the burden of identity, and it is the ego whose experiences are of a dreamlike, plastic nature. I use these terms to make the point clear. Do not let them confuse you. These portions of the self would seem topsy-turvy to you for this reason.
quotes.translation: 这些自我部分的基本身份，由你们可类比为所知的潜意识来承载。这很难理解，但请听：在这些自我部分中，是潜意识承载着身份的重担，而自我所经历的经验则具有如梦似幻、可塑可变的性质。我用这些术语是为了把观点说清楚，不要让它们混淆你们。因此，这些自我部分对你们而言会显得颠倒错乱。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: He sees or perceives only his own construction. A rereading of sessions dealing with the nature of matter will help you here. The chair created then by any given individual, and perceived by him, is an identity in that it exists at any given time, without any exact duplication. Basically, for any duplication to appear, the exact atoms and molecules would have to be used, and this is obviously impossible. The chair is an identity, and yet at no given moment is it the same chair, for already the atoms and molecules that compose it have changed, and been replaced by others. This process is explained thoroughly also in the mentioned previous sessions.
quotes.translation: 他只看见或感知到自己的建构。重读关于物质本质的课程会对你们有所帮助。那么，由任何特定个体所创造并被他感知到的椅子，是一个身份，因为它存在于任何特定时刻，没有任何精确的复制品。基本上，要出现任何复制品，就必须使用完全相同的原子和分子，而这显然是不可能的。椅子是一个身份，然而在任何特定时刻它都不是同一把椅子，因为构成它的原子和分子已经改变，并被其他原子和分子所取代。这个过程在之前提到的课程中也有详尽解释。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The next step is taken when identity is able to include within itself the intimate knowledge of all incarnations. Yet in this state the independence of the various reincarnated selves is not diminished. Each of these steps of consciousness involves identity with the inner recognition of its whole identity with All That Is.
quotes.translation: 下一步是当身份能够将所有人生的内在知识包含于自身之内时。然而在这种状态中，各个转世自我的独立性并未减弱。意识的每一步都涉及身份对其与一切万有之整体身份的内在认知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Thelped you then, before you knew me. You were quite literally waiting lor Ruburt, and this is why you did not marry earlier. The connections between you, and your development from the same prime identity, made your union an excellent one. There is a constant maintenance of identity, and a search for stability. psychologically speaking. But then identity, sure of itself, seeks to enlarge its experience for it is no longer afraid that experience will swallow it.
quotes.translation: 那么，在你认识我之前，我就帮助过你。你确实一直在等待鲁柏，这就是你为何没有早婚的原因。你们之间的联系，以及你们源自同一原始身份的发展，使你们的结合成为极佳的组合。从心理学角度来说，身份不断维持自身，并寻求稳定。但随后，自信的身份会寻求扩展其经验，因为它不再害怕经验会吞噬它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Later you may be able to follow this inner self even while the physical ego operates in its normal manner, but this is much more difficult if overall personality balance is to be maintained. It should be fairly obvious that identity hardly resides exclusively within the physically-oriented ego. In one sense, identity is always a becoming, and it can never be a static, finished thing. For it knows itself through change, and that which is finished cannot change.
quotes.translation: 日后你或许能在物质自我正常运作的同时，跟随这个内在自己，但要维持整体人格平衡，这便困难得多。显而易见，身份认同绝非仅存于物质导向的自我之中。从某种意义来说，身份认同始终处于"成为"的过程，它绝不可能是静止的、已完成的事物。因为它通过变化认识自身，而完成之物无法改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: And good evening to our friend. I do have some remarks for you and I hope that they will be helpful. You must remember to hold your head up. You should know better than to cower when you come here. I have no whip; and I would not use one if I had one. You are not sure enough of your own abilities or of your own worth. You cannot drive through physical life in the same way that you drive your car down the highway. You shall indeed, get traffic tickets and of a different kind, only you yourself give yourself tickets. You cannot force reality to give you what you want. You cannot manipulate events for egotistical purposes. You can manipulate events and you can manipulate them for your own egotistical purposes; but when you do so, you give yourself a traffic ticket. You must want what is best for your own development and the development of others rather than specifically determining what you think consciously is better for you and then trying to force or coerce fate to get this for you. If you want what is best for your own development and what is best for the development of others, then you shall attain it. It shall come to you effortlessly. I am leading up to certain issues here. You are not always aware of what is best for you on a conscious level. Often the person that you think you want or need is not the person you want or need on other levels. When you drive your car you often attempt to speed through reality as quickly as you can, and you are pleased with yourself as the driver of the vehicle. You like driving because you feel that it gets you where you want to go and quickly, and you do not mind breaking a few small rules of the road in the process. Now the small rules that you break, are indeed, minute ones. It is not that you break a specific rule; it is the attitude that allows you to break the rule; and this applies to other roads beside the physical highway. You want to get to your destination too quickly. The destination is within you. You do not have to go any place to get that destination; and it is only when you think that that destination lies elsewhere that you allow yourself to go astray. Your identity is within you and do not look for it in others. This is perhaps the strongest point of my message to you this evening, the one I would have you take to heart. When you realize that your own identity is within you will not spend energy seeking to find yourself in others. Others cannot give you a sense of worth; this is your own. Any lack is your own lack. I may perhaps deal with more specific issues this evening. But I will not discuss them until these points have been made.
quotes.translation: 晚安，我们的朋友。我有些话要对你说，希望对你有所帮助。记住要抬起头来。你来到这里时不该畏缩，这道理你本该明白。我没有鞭子；即便有也不会用。你对自己的能力和价值不够确信。你不能像在公路上开车那样横冲直撞地穿越物质生命。你确实会收到交通罚单——不过是另一种形式的——而且只有你自己能给自己开罚单。你无法强迫现实给你想要的东西。你不能为了自我目的操纵事件。你确实可以操纵事件，也可以为了自我目的操纵它们；但当你这样做时，你就是在给自己开罚单。你必须渴望对自己发展最有利、对他人发展最有利的事物，而不是刻意决定你意识层面认为对自己更好的东西，然后试图强迫或胁迫命运为你获取。若你渴望对自己发展最有利、对他人发展最有利的事物，你必将获得。它会毫不费力地来到你身边。我正引导你触及某些关键问题。你并非总能意识到什么在意识层面对你最有利。往往你自以为想要或需要的那个人，在其他层面并非你真正需要的人。当你开车时，常试图尽快穿越现实，并为自己作为驾驶者而沾沾自喜。你喜欢开车，因为觉得它能快速带你抵达目的地，过程中也不介意违反些小规则。你违反的那些小规则确实微不足道。关键不在于你违反了具体规则，而在于允许你违反规则的那种态度——这同样适用于物质公路之外的其他道路。你太急于抵达目的地。而目的地就在你之内。你无需前往任何地方去获得那个目的地；只有当你认为目的地存在于别处时，才会让自己误入歧途。你的身份认同就在你之内，不要到他人身上寻找。这或许是我今晚对你最强烈的告诫，也是我希望你铭记于心的。当你意识到自己的身份认同就在你之内时，就不会耗费精力在他人身上寻找自己。他人无法赋予你价值感；这价值感属于你自己。任何匮乏都是你自己的匮乏。今晚我或许会讨论更具体的问题。但在这些要点阐明之前，我不会深入探讨。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

source_raw.title: (I thought it a good idea that this be the end of the session, however, Seth came through again at 10:30 as the three of us sat talking about various things. A humorous repartee followed, with Seth answering our questions on a variety of subjects. Two serious notes: Alice Butts, who died last week, has already visited my father. And Dr. Instream, who would say "There is no Seth, " has not much longer to live. Also -- Bill Gallagher will not see certain truths about himself etc. Alice Butts not helping Ruth Butts ' son, etc. See Session 398.)

quotes.text: It is this dilemma, precisely between identity's constant attempts to maintain stability, and actions inherent drive for change, that results in the imbalance, the exquisite creative by-product that is consciousness of self. We have a series of creative strains. Identity must seek stability while action must seek change, yet identity could not exist without change, without action, for it is the result of action, and not apart from it but a part of it.
quotes.translation: 正是这种困境——身份认同不断寻求稳定，而行动天生渴望改变——导致了失衡，这种精妙的创造性副产品就是自我意识。我们有一系列创造性的张力。身份认同必须寻求稳定，而行动必须寻求改变，然而没有改变、没有行动，身份认同就无法存在，因为它是行动的结果，并非独立于行动，而是行动的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: You, as you think of yourself, may have trouble following such concepts, just as you would have trouble trying to follow the "future" reality of the cells within your body at this moment. (Long pause.) You must understand that in greater terms there is no big or small. There is not a giant identity and a pygmy one. Each identity is inviolate. Each also unites with others while maintaining its individuality and developing its own potential.
quotes.translation: 你，以你对自己的认知，可能难以理解这些概念，就像此刻你难以理解体内细胞"未来"的现实一样。（长停顿。）你必须明白，在更宏大的层面没有大小之分。没有庞大的身份认同和渺小的身份认同。每个身份认同都是不可侵犯的。每个身份认同在保持个体性、发展自身潜能的同时，也与其他身份认同相联结。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 725
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: In your terms, consciousness is able to hold its own sense of identity by accepting one probability, one physical life, for example, and maintaining its identity through a lifetime. Even then, certain events will be remembered and others forgotten. The consciousness also learns to handle alternate moments as it "matures." As it does so mature it forms a new, larger framework of identity, as the cell forms into an organ on another level.
quotes.translation: 用你们的话说，意识通过接受一个可能性（例如一次物质生命）来维持自身身份认同感，并在一生中保持其身份认同。即便如此，某些事件会被记住，另一些则被遗忘。随着意识"成熟"，它也学会处理交替时刻。当它真正成熟时，会形成一个新的、更宏大的身份认同框架，就像细胞在另一层面形成器官一样。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 681
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: In a larger manner, the identity of the soul can be seen from the same viewpoint. It knows who it is, and is far more certain of its identity, indeed, than your physical self is of its identity. And yet now where in this electromagnetic energy field can the identity of the soul as such be found?
quotes.translation: 从更宏大的视角来看，灵魂的身份认同也可用同样的观点来审视。它知道自己的身份，而且比物质自我对自己的身份认同要确定得多。然而，在这电磁能量场中，灵魂的身份认同究竟存在于何处呢？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Basically there are no real divisions to the self, but for the sake of explanation we must speak of them in those terms. First of all you had the inner self, the creative dreaming self--composed, again, of units of consciousness, awareized energy that forms your identity, and that formed the identities of the earliest earth inhabitants. These inner selves formed their own dream bodies about them, as previously explained, but the dream bodies did not have to have physical reactions. They were free of gravity and space, and of time.
quotes.translation: 自我本质上并无真正划分，但为便于解释，我们不得不以此类术语论述。首先，你拥有内在自我——那具创造力的、做梦的自我——它同样由意识单位、形成你身份的能量觉知构成，也形成了最早地球居民的身份。如先前所述，这些内在自我在其周围形成了自己的梦体，但梦体无需具备物理反应。它们不受重力、空间与时间的束缚。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When you maintain that identity is dependent upon the duration of the physical body, you are taking it for granted that the physical body is one complete thing, more or less rigid in form, and permanent within a certain perspective. You know however that the physical body is not one thing in those terms, and that the stuff of which it is composed is forever coming and going, arriving and departing, and yet identity is maintained.
quotes.translation: 当你坚持身份依赖于肉体的存续时，你便理所当然地认为肉体是一个完整之物，形态大致固定，并在特定视角下具有恒常性。然而你深知，肉体并非此类术语中的单一实体，构成它的物质不断来去、聚散，而身份却得以维系。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The dreaming self as you conceive of it, however, is but a shadow of its own reality, for the dreaming self is a psychological point of reference and, in your terms, [of] continuity, that brings together all portions of your identity. Of its deeper nature, only the most developed are aware. It represents, in other words, one strong uniting facet of your entire identity. Its experiences are as vivid and its "personality" as rich -- in fact richer -- in context as the physical personality you know.
quotes.translation: 然而，你所构想的做梦自我仅是它自身实相的一个影子，因为做梦自我是心理上的参照点，就你们而言也是连续性的参照点，它将你身份的所有部分汇聚在一起。唯有最发达者才能觉察其更深层的本质。换言之，它代表了你整个身份中一个强大的统一面向。其体验之生动、"人格"之丰富——事实上比你所知的物质人格更为丰富——在情境中亦然。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "You" presently have a once-centered consciousness, in that "you" close off from your experience these other stages of consciousness in which other portions of your entire identity are intimately involved. These other stages of consciousness create their own realities as you create your own. The realities are, therefore, byproducts of consciousness itself. If you could become aware of these, they might appear to be other places to you, rather than realms or fields of different kinds of activities. If you probe into these realms you will be forced to perceive them with the root assumptions of your own system, translating feelings of warmth and comfort, for example, into images of warm shelters or buildings, or feelings of fear into images of demons.
quotes.translation: "你"目前拥有一个单点聚焦的意识，因为"你"从自身经验中隔绝了其他意识阶段——这些阶段中，你整个身份的其他部分正密切参与。这些其他意识阶段创造着各自的实相，正如你创造自己的实相。因此，实相本身就是意识的副产品。若能觉察它们，它们在你眼中可能更像是其他地点，而非不同活动类型的领域或场域。若你深入探究这些领域，你将被迫用自己系统的根本假设去感知它们，例如将温暖舒适的感觉转化为温暖庇护所或建筑物的意象，或将恐惧感转化为恶魔的意象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You read your own consciousness now in a kind of vertical fashion, identifying only with certain portions of it, and it seems to you that any other organization of perception, any other recognition of identity, would quite necessarily negate your own or render it inoperable. In the beginning of the world there were numberless groupings, however, and affiliations of consciousness, many other organizations of identity that were recognized, as well as the kind of psychological orientation you have now--but [your] kind of orientation was not the paramount one. While, generally speaking, earth's species existed from the beginning in the forms by which you now know them, consciousness of species was quite different, and all species were much more intimately related through various kinds of identification that have since gone into the underground of awareness.
quotes.translation: 你此刻以某种纵向方式解读自己的意识，仅认同其中特定部分，因而对你而言，任何其他感知组织方式、任何其他身份认知，都必然否定你的身份或使其失效。然而在世界之初，存在着无数意识的组合与联结，许多其他身份组织方式被认知，也存在着你现今拥有的这种心理取向——但[你的]这种取向并非首要的。虽然大体而言，地球物种自始便以你所知的形态存在，但物种的意识却截然不同，所有物种通过后来沉入意识底层的各种认同方式，保持着更为密切的关联。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The basic consciousness is never physical. Yet within your system it must collect experience within the physical system, hence the physical body. But experience itself is not physical, and cannot be contained within physical matter. Therefore this experience, collected within the physical field, is held in codified form by this inner self or astral identity. It is only by understanding the connection between the physical and nonphysical self, and the communication systems that operate here, that the true nature of human personality can be studied. While we are dealing now with your own species, it should be realized that all consciousness also possesses its own astral identity. The inner senses are part of this nonphysical self. They allow the personality to retain its relationship with nonphysical reality, permitting the material self to focus within its earthly environment.
quotes.translation: 基本意识绝非物质性的。然而在你的系统内，它必须在物质系统中收集经验，故而有肉体。但经验本身并非物质，也无法被物质所容纳。因此，在物质场域中收集的这些经验，以编码形式被这个内在自我或星光身份所持有。唯有理解物质自我与非物质自我之间的联系，以及在此运作的沟通系统，才能研究人类人格的真实本质。虽然我们此刻讨论的是你们这个物种，但应认识到所有意识也拥有其自身的星光身份。内在感官属于这个非物质自我的一部分。它们允许人格保持与非物质实相的关系，使物质自我得以聚焦于其地球环境之中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: Identity is no longer limited to the outer ego alone. The outer ego is now familiar with the whole self, or the entire identity, and has available to it strength of which it was not previously aware. In periods of exuberance, when you are working well, and your health is extraordinarily good, when you are able to remember and manipulate your dreams, then such periods are signs of the emergence of this new consciousness.
quotes.translation: 身份不再局限于外在自我。外在自我如今已熟悉整体自性或完整身份，并获得了先前未曾察觉的力量。当你处于精力充沛的时期——工作顺遂、健康极佳、能记忆并操控梦境时——这些阶段便是新意识浮现的征兆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Identity always becomes part of that which it perceives, however, and so it constantly forms new gestalts, while accepting as itself a larger variety of experiences. Therefore identity grows, and as I have told you there are no limitations to it. Therefore we are forming new identities, and you have already accepted as part of yourself experiences which previously would have been considered alien, and not accepted.
quotes.translation: 然而身份始终会融入它所感知的对象，因此它不断形成新的完形，同时将更丰富的体验接纳为自身的一部分。于是身份得以成长，正如我曾告知的，它不存在任何限制。我们正在形成新的身份，而你们已开始将先前视为异己、拒绝接纳的体验，纳入自身认知范畴。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Now, my welcome to our friend here, and we will for now continue with our discussion. The term, identity, like many other terms, is limited, for you think of identity as being one indivisible unit. Now. Identity is not dependent upon any sort of structure, as you know it.
quotes.translation: 现在，欢迎我们的新朋友加入。我们将继续探讨。如同许多术语，"身份"一词本身具有局限性，因为你们将身份视为不可分割的单一单元。但事实上，身份并不依赖于你们所认知的任何结构形态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Your main sense of identity is involved with your physical body, so that it is, for example, extremely difficult for you to imagine yourself without it, or outside it, or in any way disconnected from it. Form is the result of concentrated energy, the pattern for it caused by vividly directed emotional or psychic idea images. The intensity is all important. If you have, for example, a highly vivid desire to be somewhere else, then without realizing it consciously a pseudophysical form, identical with your own, may appear in that very spot. The desire will carry the imprint of your personality and image, even though you remain unaware of the image or its appearance in the other location.
quotes.translation: 你们对身份的主要认知与肉体紧密相连，因此极难想象脱离肉体、超越肉体或与之割裂的状态。形态是能量凝聚的结果，其模式由强烈定向的情感或心灵意念图像塑造。强度至关重要。例如，若你怀有强烈渴望前往某处，即便未意识到，一个与你完全相同的拟物理形态可能已出现在该地点。这份渴望会携带你人格与形象的印记，即便你对此影像及其在异地的显现毫无察觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The larger self, then, seeds itself in time. In this process no identity is lost and no identity is the same, yet all are interrelated. So you can theoretically expand your consciousness to include the knowledge of your past lives, though those lives were yours and not yours. They have a common root, as next year's leaves have a common root with the leaves now of this plant (pointing again to the begonia).
quotes.translation: 因此，更大的自性在时间中播撒自身。在此过程中，没有身份会消失，也没有身份完全相同，但所有身份皆相互关联。理论上你们可以扩展意识，涵盖对前世的认知——尽管那些生命既属于你又不属于你。它们共享同一根源，如同这株秋海棠（再次指向盆栽）明年的叶片与今时的叶片同源。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 731
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: It is energy concentrated to a degree quite unbelievable to you. It contains potentials unlimited, but it must work out its own identity and form its own worlds. It carries within it the burden of all being. Within it are personality potentials beyond your comprehension. Remember, this is your own soul or entity I am speaking of, as well as soul or entity in general. You are one manifestation of your own soul. How many of you would want to limit your reality, your entire reality, to the experience you now know? You do this when you imagine that your present self is your entire personality, or insist that your identity be maintained unchanged through an endless eternity.
quotes.translation: 这是以你们难以想象的强度凝聚的能量。它蕴含无限潜能，但必须发展自身身份并构建自己的世界。它承载着所有存在的重负，其中包含超乎你们理解的人格潜能。请记住，我谈论的是你们自己的灵魂或存有，也是普遍意义上的灵魂或存有。你们是自身灵魂的一种显化。有多少人愿意将自身实相——全部实相——局限于此刻所知的体验？当你们想象当前自我即完整人格，或坚持身份必须永恒不变时，便是在做这样的事。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You are truly multidimensional personalities, as I have said before. At some point in your development you will become more and more aware of the true nature of your identity. There is, for example, a part of you who is very aware of the pulsations you have just been discussing, and who is aware of the pulse-like nature of memory. When the pulse is in this physical reality, then you, as you know yourselves, have memory of this existence. When the pulse is in another dimension, there is memory of that existence. Now a portion of your entire identity has memory of both. The entire personality structure dwells in many dimensions, and simultaneously.
quotes.translation: 如我先前所言，你们确实是多维度的人格。在你们发展的某个阶段，你们会越来越意识到自己身份的真实本质。例如，你们有一部分非常清楚你们刚刚讨论的脉动，也明白记忆的脉动本质。当脉动处于这个物质实相时，你们所认知的自我便拥有对此存在的记忆；当脉动处于另一个维度时，便拥有对那个存在的记忆。而你们整体身份的一部分，同时拥有这两者的记忆。整个的人格结构栖居于许多维度之中，且是同时进行的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Within your particular plane of activity, and speaking practically, no one fully or completely can use all the energy available to them, or completely materialize the inner sensed identity that is multidimensional. This inner identity is the blueprint however against which you judge, ultimately, your physical actions. You strive to express as best you can the entire potential that is within you.
quotes.translation: 在你们特定的活动层面，且从实际角度来说，没有人能完全或彻底运用所有可用的能量，或完全物质化那内在感知到的、多维度的身份。然而，这个内在身份是蓝图，你们最终会以此评判自己的物质行为。你们尽力表达内在的全部潜能。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: A great artist in any field or in any time instinctively feels a private personhood that is greater than the particular sexual identity. As long as you equate identity with your sexuality, you will limit the potentials of the individual and of the species. Each person will generally find it easier to operate as male or female, lesbian or homosexual, but each person is primarily bisexual. Bisexuality implies parenthood as much as it implies lesbian or homosexual relationships. Again, here, sexual encounters are a natural part of love's expression, but they are not the limit of love's expression.
quotes.translation: 任何领域或任何时代的伟大艺术家，都会本能地感受到一个比特定性别身份更宏大的私人人格。只要你们将身份等同于性取向，就会限制个体和物种的潜能。每个人通常会发现以男性或女性、女同性恋或男同性恋的身份运作较为容易，但每个人本质上都是双性的。双性恋既暗示着同性恋关系，也暗示着为人父母。再次强调，性接触是爱之表达的自然部分，但并非爱的极限。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner self chooses from its available potential personalities the one that it finds most adequate. Sometimes it simply makes an error, for the inner self is not a perfected thing, any more than the ego is. But identity does exist, and the ego is but a pseudo identity formed for utilitarian reasons, and as such it is of course a part of the basic identity from which it springs.
quotes.translation: 内在自我从可用的潜在人格中选择它认为最合适的一个。有时它只是犯了错误，因为内在自我并非完美之物，正如自我也不是完美的一样。但身份确实存在，而自我不过是为实用目的形成的伪身份，因此它自然是从其所源出的基本身份的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: You cannot touch the action. You cannot touch the action, now, of your own arm as you write. You see the results of the action. You can feel effects of the action, but you cannot directly perceive the action itself. Since identity is dependent upon action, then it should be seen that it is impossible for an identity to attain stability, since total stability would destroy it.
quotes.translation: 你无法触摸行动。此刻，当你书写时，你无法触摸自己手臂的行动。你看到行动的结果，能感受到行动的效果，但无法直接感知行动本身。既然身份依赖于行动，那么就应该明白，身份不可能获得稳定，因为完全的稳定会摧毁它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The soul is not frightened for its identity. It is sure of itself. It ever seeks. It is not afraid of being overwhelmed by experience or perception. If you had a more thorough understanding of the nature of identity you would not, for example, fear telepathy, for behind this concern is the worry that your identity will be swept away by the suggestions or thoughts of others.
quotes.translation: 灵魂并不为它的身份而恐惧。它对自己确信无疑。它永远在探索。它不害怕被经验或感知淹没。如果你们对身份的本质有更透彻的理解，就不会害怕心灵感应，因为这种担忧背后是害怕自己的身份会被他人的建议或思想冲垮。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: All matter is based upon the units mentioned, with their unpredictability and their propensity for exploring all probabilities. Even your atomic structure, then, is poised between probabilities. If this is true, then obviously "you" are aware of only one small probable portion of yourself -- and this portion you protect as your identity (underlined). If you think of it as simply a focus taken by "your" greater identity, then you will be able to follow what I am saying without feeling puny by contrast, or lost.6 The focus that you have is indeed inviolate.
quotes.translation: 所有物质都基于前述单位，它们具有不可预测性及探索所有可能性的倾向。就连你们的原子结构也悬于可能性之间。若果真如此，那么显然"你"只意识到自身极小一部分的可能性——而你将这部分保护起来视为你的身份（下划线强调）。若你将其视为"更伟大身份"所采取的聚焦点，便能理解我所说的话，而不会因对比感到渺小或迷失。你所拥有的这个聚焦点确实神圣不可侵犯。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 682
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: Its sense of identity spontaneously goes beyond the change of its own form. It has no ego to cut the "I" identification short. Creatures without the compartment of the ego can easily follow their own identities beyond any changes of form. The inner self is aware of this integrity of identity, but the ego focused so securely in physical reality cannot afford this luxury.
quotes.translation: 其身份感自发超越自身形态的变化。它没有自我意识来切断"我"的认同。没有自我意识这个隔间的生物，能轻易跟随自身身份超越任何形态变化。内在自我知晓这种身份的完整性，但如此牢固聚焦于物质实相的自我意识却负担不起这种奢侈。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (First I thought of Seth's assertion in the 92nd session for September 28, 1964, that "trees have their dreams"; [in Volume 1, see the quotations in Note 1 for Session 698]. Then I remembered that he'd come through with extensive material on tree consciousness in a much earlier session. With Jane's help the next day I found that information in the 18th session, which had been held on January 22, 1964. Most of that session is unpublished, although someday we'd like to print it in full. It contains many intriguing ideas -- as, for example: "A tree knows a human being also ... [yet it] does not even build up an image of a man, which is why this is so difficult to explain ... And the same tree will recognize the same man who passes it by each day."
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The individuals continue to exist, and express themselves according to their development in other fields not connected with yours, when they are finished on your plane. No particular identity is ever lost. If so the whole process would be meaningless. And in other ways to lesser degrees, through abstract thought, through art of any kind, the physical human being, having been formed by consciousness, in his own way then working through and with matter, constructs other fields or planes of attraction, which according to their abilities expand.
quotes.translation: 个体在你们层面完成体验后，会继续存在并根据自身发展，在其它与你们无关的领域表达自己。没有任何特定身份会失落。若真如此，整个进程将毫无意义。通过抽象思维、各类艺术等其它较低程度的方式，由意识形成的物质人类，以其自身方式透过物质运作，建构出其它吸引场域或层面，这些场域按其能力不断扩展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: I want you to try and imagine a situation in which (long pause) there exists a psychological force that includes within its capabilities the ability to act simultaneously on the most microscopic and the most macroscopic levels; that can form within itself (long pause, eyes closed) a million separate inviolate unique identities, and that can still operate as a part of those identities, and as a larger unit that is their source--in which case it is a wave from which the particles emerge. That description fits our units of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 我要你们尝试想象这样一种情境（长停顿）：存在一种心理力量，其能力包含同时作用于最微观与最宏观层面的能力；能在自身之内（长停顿，闭眼）形成百万个各自独立、神圣不可侵犯的独特身份，同时仍能作为这些身份的一部分运作，并作为它们的源头——那个更大的单位——运作；在此情况下，它是一道涌现出粒子的波。这个描述符合我们的意识单位。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Some of these may involve the thoughts of what you would call a reincarnational self, focused in another period of history as you know it. You may instead, "pick up" an event in which a probable self is involved, according to your inclination, your psychic suppleness, your curiosity, your desire for knowledge. In other words, you may become aware of a far greater reality than you now know, use abilities that you do not realize you possess, know beyond all doubt that your own consciousness and identity is independent of the world in which you now focus your primary attention. If all of that were not true, I would not be writing this book and you would not be reading it.
quotes.translation: 其中某些可能涉及你们所谓的转世自我的思想，聚焦于你们所知历史中的另一个时期。根据你的倾向、心灵柔韧度、好奇心及求知欲，你反而可能"捕捉"到涉及可能性自我的事件。换言之，你可能意识到远比现在所知更伟大的实相，运用你尚未察觉自身拥有的能力，确凿无疑地知晓自己的意识与身份独立于你目前主要聚焦的世界。若非如此，我就不会写这本书，你也不会读它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The whole self or identity is aware of the experiences of all the egos, and since one identity forms these egos there is bound to be some similarities between them, and characteristics. The material that I have given you on reincarnation in the past is quite valid, particularly for working purposes, but it is a highly simplified version of what actually occurs.
quotes.translation: 整体自我或身份知晓所有自我意识的体验，由于单一身份形成这些自我意识，它们之间必然存在某些相似性与特征。我过去给予你们的转世资料相当有效，尤其适用于实际运作，但这只是实际发生情况的极度简化版本。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: It helps form the new individual, and then it must withdraw. The new self unit must be free and not hampered by the demands that could otherwise be put upon it. The new individual has a deeply buried memory of its past lives, but the personal consciousness of the last reincarnated self must not be superimposed upon this new identity. The new personality, in its small astral body, does visit with other portions of the entire identity. It is even given lessons of a kind, but it is very much its own self.
quotes.translation: 它协助形成新的个体，然后必须撤离。新的自体单元必须自由，不受可能加诸其上的要求所阻碍。新个体深埋着对前世的记忆，但上一世转世自我的个人意识不得叠加于这个新身份之上。新人格在其微小的星光体中，确实会与整个身份的其他部分交流，甚至接受某种课程，但它仍然是它自己。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Now, if you will follow me closely you will obtain a hint at least of the structure of the inner self. It is composed, that is, each inner self is composed, structurally of a particular range of electrical intensities with which various personalities have their identities insured, since their identity is composed of particular intensities within the range.
quotes.translation: 现在，如果你们仔细跟随我，至少会获得关于内在自体结构的一点提示。每个内在自体在结构上由特定范围的电磁强度组成，各种人格的身份借此得到保障，因为它们的身份由该范围内的特定强度构成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Egos concept of itself as a rock of unchanging identity is highly ludicrous, since its perceptions constantly change it from what it was. It is not a thing proceeding, by any means. This is not to deny the human aspects of the ego, for the ego is also somewhat like a light carried in front of the inner self, a light that gives meaning to the physical universe and to its objects. It enables the inner self to manipulate within physical reality. It translates outer data to the inner personality.
quotes.translation: 自我将自己视为永恒不变的磐石身份，这种观念极为可笑，因为它的感知不断将其从原来的状态改变。它绝非一个持续前进的事物。这并非否定自我的人类面向，因为自我也像内在自体前方的一盏灯，赋予物质宇宙及其物体以意义。它使内在自体能在物质实相中运作，并将外在数据翻译给内在人格。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: This density is extremely important, for it is a density of intensities. And it is the infinite variety and gradations of intensity that makes all identities possible, and all gestalts, all identities in terms of personalities and fields and universes. It is this density, this infinite variety of intensity, which allows for both identity and of change.
quotes.translation: 这种密度极为重要，因为它是强度的密度。正是强度的无限变化与层级，使得所有身份、所有完形、所有以人格、场域和宇宙形式存在的身份成为可能。正是这种密度、这种强度的无限变化，同时允许了身份与改变的存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: So if you want to know what my environment is like, you will have to understand what I am. In order to explain, I shall have to speak about the nature of consciousness in general. In doing so I shall end up telling you much about yourself. The inner portions of your identity are already aware of much that I will tell you. Part of my purpose is to acquaint your egotistical self with knowledge that is already known to a larger portion of your own consciousness, that you have long ignored.
quotes.translation: 因此，若你想知道我的环境如何，就必须先理解我是什么。为了解释，我将不得不谈论一般意识的本质。在此过程中，我最终会告诉你许多关于你自己的事。你身份的内在部分早已知晓我将讲述的许多内容。我的部分目的是让你那自我性的自己熟悉那些已被你自身意识更大部分所知、却长期忽视的知识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He literally could not operate until he assimilated these new experiences or denied them, in the main. Your encouragement was essential, and your support and encouragement is now. You are separate individuals and quite separate personalities, and you will continue to exist as separate identities. Nevertheless you are also part of other identities. And you two, you two form a gestalt, so that working together you have more knowledge, ability and information than you have separately.
quotes.translation: 他基本上无法运作，直到他同化或否定了这些新体验。你的鼓励至关重要，而你现在仍需要支持与鼓励。你们是独立的个体，是相当独立的人格，并将继续作为独立的身份存在。然而，你们也是其他身份的一部分。你们俩，你们俩形成了一个完形，因此共同合作时，你们拥有的知识、能力与信息比单独时更多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Using this as an analogy, you are a part of your psyche or your soul, dwelling within it, easily following your own sense of identity even though that psyche also contains other identities beside the one that you think of as your own. You draw sustenance from the world, and grow through its medium. You contribute your abilities and experience, helping to form the world's civilization and culture. To some strong degree you bear the same kind of relationship to your own psyche.
quotes.translation: 以此类推，你是自己心灵或灵魂的一部分，栖居其中，即使那心灵也包含着你认为属于自己的身份之外的其他身份，你仍能轻易追随自己的身份感。你从世界汲取养分，并透过其媒介成长。你贡献自身的能力与经验，协助塑造世界的文明与文化。在相当程度上，你与自己的心灵也维持着类似的关系。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. A strong part of the ego identity is based upon the continuity of creative achievement, which springs from the subconscious. If this were severely tampered with, the ego itself would suffer. Now give us a moment on the clairvoyant question. If the suggestion is given daily as I told you, there will be no great difficulty in any case. There is at present no outstanding clairvoyant data that is affecting his symptoms or causing any new symptoms, outside of those given.
quotes.translation: 现在。自我身份感的一大部分建立于创造性成就的连续性之上，而这连续性源自潜意识。若此连续性遭到严重干扰，自我本身将受损害。现在，关于千里眼的问题，请给我们一点时间。若如我所说每日给予暗示，任何情况下都不会有太大困难。目前除了已给出的那些之外，并无显著影响其症状或引发新症状的千里眼数据。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Now your physical body is a field of energy with a certain form, however, and when someone asks you your name, your lips speak it -- and yet the name does not belong to the atoms and molecules in the lips that utter the syllables. The name has meaning only to you. Within your body you cannot put your finger upon your own identity. If you could travel within your body, you could not find where your identity resides, yet you say, "This is my body," and, "This is my name."
quotes.translation: 然而，你们的肉体是一个具有特定形态的能量场。当有人问起你的名字，你的嘴唇会说出它——但这个名字并不属于发出音节的嘴唇中的原子与分子。名字只对你有意义。在你的身体里，你无法用手指指出自己的身份。即使你能在体内旅行，也找不到身份所在之处，但你仍会说："这是我的身体"，以及"这是我的名字"。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Such a belief in extinction, such a certainty that identity is about to be blotted out in the next moment, is a severe psychological experience, that in itself can bring about unfortunate reactions. What happens instead is that you find consciousness quite intact, and its expression far less limited than it was before. Joseph chose subconsciously to interrupt those methods of expression he was using at the time simply so that their interference would claim due notice.
quotes.translation: 这种相信消亡的信念，这种确信身份即将在下一刻被抹除的确定感，是一种严重的心理体验，其本身就可能引发不幸的反应。实际发生的情况是，你会发现意识完好无损，且其表达方式远不如先前受限。约瑟夫潜意识地选择中断当时正在使用的那些表达方式，纯粹是为了让这些干扰引起应有的注意。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now. These other selves are more freewheeling. There are indeed limitations inherent within their structure, but in all cases any given identity is more than the dimension in which it finds itself. Its limitations may be great, but the limitations are set not by the identity's nature but by the dimension in which it exists.
quotes.translation: 现在。这些其他的自我更为自由奔放。其结构确实存在固有的限制，但在所有情况下，任何给定的身份都超越其所在维度。其限制可能很大，但这些限制并非由身份的本质所设定，而是由它存在的维度所决定。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)
quotes.source_file: ES-5-The Early Sessions-Book 5 of The Seth Material(199-239).json

quotes.text: This incidentally is the least acceptable survival pattern. The ego may be strong enough to insist upon its old prerogatives of control, yet too inflexible to adjust to the new conditions. It may accept the challenge of great change on the other hand, and allow a reorganization of psychological processes, in which case identity is not only saved but renewed. The ego is then in command after some struggle of generic frameworks, in a way not possible to it earlier.
quotes.translation: 这顺便一提是最不可接受的存活模式。自我可能足够强大，坚持其旧有的控制特权，却又过于僵化而无法适应新条件。另一方面，它也可能接受巨大变化的挑战，允许心理过程的重组，在这种情况下，身份不仅得以保存，而且获得更新。自我随后在经历某种通用框架的挣扎后，以先前不可能的方式重新掌控局面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Because of the great organizing nature of these basic units, there are also psychological structures that are quite capable of holding their own identities while being aware of any given number of probable selves. Life after death has great meaning in your reality, because death is a part of it. Your greater reality obviously transcends both your births and your deaths. The idea of one universe alone is basically nonsensical. Your reality must be seen in its relationship to others.8 Otherwise you are always caught in questions like "How did the universe begin?" or "When will it end?" All systems are constantly being created.
quotes.translation: 由于这些基本单位具有强大的组织性，也存在能够充分保持自身身份认同、同时感知任意数量可能自我的心理结构。在你们的实相中，死后生命具有重大意义，因为死亡是其组成部分。你们更宏大的实相显然超越了你们的出生与死亡。单一宇宙的概念本质上毫无意义。你们的实相必须在其与其他实相的关联中被看待，否则你们永远会被困在"宇宙如何起源？"或"它何时终结？"这类问题中。所有系统都在持续被创造着。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 682
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: The Private Probable Man, The Private Probable Woman, The Species in Probabilities, And Blueprints for Realities

quotes.text: "That identity of Billy's remains vital, known to itself whether or not it is reactivated in your terms. This is not necessarily always the case -- and there is great variation -- but Billy identified with 'the larger organization' of the litter [that is, with his brothers and sisters, all of whom are also dead], and the consciousnesses of that litter are now together. They are forming a gestalt, where the five consciousnesses will merge to form a new identity."
quotes.translation: "比利的那个身份认同依然充满活力，无论它是否按你们的说法被重新激活，它自身都知晓自身。这并非总是如此——存在巨大差异——但比利认同于那窝幼崽的'更大组织'（即认同于他的兄弟姐妹们，他们也都已去世），那窝幼崽的意识如今聚在一起。它们正在形成一个完形，五个意识将融合以形成一个新的身份认同。"
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: From Session 839 for March 7: "The quality of identity is far more mysterious than you understand, for you assign an identity in a blanket fashion, say, to each living thing. Now your dead cat, Billy, exists in the following manner:
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You could not appreciate nor understand, nor can I, the nature of identity as it is known in the overall. What we know of identity represents fragments and splinters that we call ourselves. These are part however of the primary identity.
